title: 山核桃大街谋杀案
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\121-山核桃大街谋杀案-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Mrs. Nikolaitis instructs Mrs. Hubbard to have a cabinet door repaired and a brighter light bulb installed in the hallway, insisting that the police cover the repair costs. Mrs. Nikolaitis expresses a fear that she is being followed, though Mrs. Hubbard suggests her employer tends to exaggerate. After leaving the house and walking down Walnut Street, Mrs. Nikolaitis enters "The Queen's Necklace" pub. While drinking brandy, she broods over the police discovering her secret stash of alcohol and worries that Girolamo and others will spread this information. After being interrupted by an acquaintance, she attempts to walk home, but the alcohol makes her dizzy. She stumbles near a mailbox and collapses; a clerk alerts Officer Bote, who finds her and concludes she is merely intoxicated.

Meanwhile, Hercule Poirot examines four canvas backpacks, including the one he purchased from Mr. Hicks. Using a paring knife, he slices them open to inspect their construction and notes that the Hicks backpack contains a wavy hardboard lining. Poirot calls Inspector Sharpe to request the specific dates and times of past police investigations on Chestnut Street. Sharpe provides three instances: an investigation into seditious pamphlets on December 18th, an investigation into Montague Jones on February 24th, and an investigation into William Robinson on March 6th. Following the call, Poirot reviews a revised list of items provided by Mrs. Habberton, which includes a stethoscope, a diamond ring, and boric acid.
